public-preflight-push.decision: Do not push the package/outreach preflight commit yet.

no_approval_executed.boundary: local source metadata only; no package build upload or registry state
local wheel/install smoke only; no network dependency resolution and no registry upload
draft copy and response policy only; no post, message, issue, pull request, or maintainer contact

approval_required.requires_owner_approval_because: chooses external registry state and package-name ownership path

options.label: TestPyPI first, then PyPI
options.reason: Safest first package path; validates registry rendering and install behavior before consuming the official PyPI version.

options.label: Direct PyPI
options.reason: Fast but too risky for first package release because bad PyPI versions require yank plus patch release.

options.label: GitHub Packages only
options.reason: Keeps distribution near source but is weaker for normal Python discovery.

options.label: Source-only GitHub Release
options.reason: Lowest rollback risk, but higher user friction.

approval_required.requires_owner_approval_because: selects credential and CI trust model

options.label: PyPI trusted publishing
options.reason: Avoids local long-lived API tokens and gives an auditable CI path.

options.label: Project-scoped API token with twine
options.reason: Works when trusted publishing is unavailable, but requires explicit token handling.

approval_required.requires_owner_approval_because: creates public communication state outside the repository

options.label: GitHub Release only
options.reason: Already live, lowest risk, but limited reach.

options.label: Package-first announcement
options.reason: Best user path after package verification: install, run doctor, inspect report.

options.label: Technical article
options.reason: Good fit for explaining authority layers, but needs exact-copy review.

options.label: Maintainer outreach
options.reason: Too easy to imply evaluation, endorsement, or compatibility.
